prompt: Using the official openai Python package, write a short script that asks gpt-4o to explain list comprehensions in one paragraph and prints the model's reply.
I just pip-installed the latest openai library. Show me minimal Python code to create the client, send one question to a chat model, and print the answer text.
Write a Python function ask(question: str) -> str that uses the OpenAI SDK's chat completions endpoint with the system prompt 'You are terse.' and returns the assistant's reply.
Python + openai SDK: stream a chat completion for 'write a limerick about type hints' and print the text as it arrives, without extra newlines between chunks.
How do I stream tokens from gpt-4o-mini in Python using the openai library? Give me complete runnable code that prints each piece of text as soon as it is generated.
Using the OpenAI Python SDK, build a tiny terminal chat loop that keeps the conversation history in a list and streams every assistant reply to stdout as it is produced.
Use the OpenAI Responses API from Python to ask a model for three startup name ideas and print the output text.
Show Python code that calls OpenAI's Responses API with instructions telling the model to answer like a pirate, then prints the response's output text.
With the openai Python SDK's Responses API, stream the answer to 'count from 1 to 10' and print each text delta as it arrives.

Write Python code with the openai package that gives a chat model a get_weather(city) function tool, asks what the weather is in Oslo, and prints the name and arguments of the tool call the model returns.

Using the OpenAI SDK in Python, implement the full function-calling round trip: the model requests lookup_stock(symbol), your code runs a local stub, you send the tool result back, and you print the model's final answer.
OpenAI Python SDK question: how do I pass a Pydantic model as a function tool so the tool-call arguments come back already parsed into the model class? Show runnable code.

Using the openai Python library, define a Pydantic model Recipe(name: str, ingredients: list[str], minutes: int) and get the model to return a parsed Recipe object for 'a quick pasta dish'. Print the parsed object.
Python openai SDK: classify the tweet 'ugh, my flight got cancelled again' into JSON with fields sentiment and confidence, forcing the output to match a JSON schema. Print the parsed result.
Write a Python function embed(texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]] that uses the OpenAI SDK with the text-embedding-3-small model.
Using the openai package in Python, compute embeddings for two sentences and print their cosine similarity. Implement the cosine similarity with the math module, no numpy.
Show an asyncio example that uses the OpenAI Python SDK's async client to send three chat requests concurrently with asyncio.gather and prints each answer.
I need an async generator in Python, using the openai library, that streams a chat completion and yields the text chunks as they arrive. Show the full code including client setup.
Python code with the openai SDK: base64-encode a local image file and ask a vision-capable model what is in the photo. Print the answer.
Using the OpenAI Python SDK, ask the model to describe the image at https://example.com/cat.png in one sentence and print the reply.
Using the anthropic Python SDK, send 'Why is the sky blue?' to Claude and print the reply. Include the client setup.

Minimal Python example with the official anthropic package: one user message to Claude, then print just the plain text of the response. The response object shape confuses me, so show exactly how to get the text out.
Write a Python function chat(history: list, user_msg: str) -> str that calls Claude through the anthropic SDK with a system prompt and the running message history, and returns the assistant's text.
With the anthropic Python library, send Claude a multi-turn conversation (user, assistant, user) in a single request and print the text of the final answer.
Python anthropic SDK: stream Claude's answer to 'tell me a short story about a robot' and print the text as it arrives.
Using the anthropic package's streaming helper in Python, print each text delta from Claude as it arrives, and after the stream ends print the complete final message object.
How do I stream a long Claude response in Python with the anthropic SDK's context manager so tokens print as they come? Full runnable code please.
Write an async Python script with the anthropic SDK that streams Claude's reply to a question and flush-prints every piece of text immediately.
Using the anthropic Python SDK, define a get_time(timezone) tool, ask Claude what time it is in Tokyo, and print the tool name and input Claude wants to use.

Anthropic SDK in Python: implement the whole tool-use loop. Claude asks for a calculator tool, your code evaluates the expression, you send the tool result back, and you print Claude's final text answer.
Show Python code with the anthropic package where Claude chooses between two tools, search_web and get_news, and is forced to use one of them. Print which tool it picked and with what input.
With the anthropic Python SDK, stream a response in which Claude calls a weather tool, and print the partial JSON of the tool input while it is still streaming in.
Using the anthropic SDK in Python, extract product, quantity, and unit_price from 'Order 3 lattes at $4.50 each' into a Pydantic model and print the parsed object.
Python + anthropic: make Claude return strictly a JSON object with keys city, country, population for the question 'largest city in Norway', then json.loads it and print the dict.
With the anthropic Python SDK, read a local JPEG, base64-encode it, send it to Claude with the question 'Describe this image', and print the answer.
Anthropic Python SDK: pass an image to Claude by URL together with the question 'How many people are in this picture?' and print the response text.
Write an asyncio script using the anthropic SDK's async client that sends three different prompts to Claude concurrently and prints each result.
Using the async anthropic client in Python, write a coroutine ask_claude(q: str) -> str that returns Claude's text answer and applies a 30-second timeout.
Python anthropic SDK: enable extended thinking with a 2000-token budget for a logic puzzle, then print Claude's thinking blocks and its final text answer separately.
Using the anthropic Python SDK, count how many input tokens a request would use for the user message 'summarize War and Peace' with Claude, without generating a response.
Using the ollama Python package, send 'Why is the sky blue?' to llama3.2 and print the answer.
Python ollama library: write a small REPL chat loop with llama3.2 that keeps the conversation history in a messages list.
With the ollama Python package, stream llama3.2's response to 'explain DNS briefly' and print the chunks as they arrive.
Ollama Python SDK: stream a completion for a haiku prompt and print each part immediately, without extra newlines.
Using the ollama Python library's generate function, complete the prompt 'The capital of France is' with llama3.2 and print only the response text.

Python ollama package: define a Pydantic model Pet(name: str, species: str, age: int), make llama3.2 fill it from a free-text description using the format parameter, and validate the reply with Pydantic.
With ollama in Python, constrain a local model's output to a JSON schema with fields title (string) and tags (array of strings), then print the validated result.
Using the ollama Python package, get embedding vectors for the sentences 'I love pizza' and 'I hate rain' from an embedding model and print the length of each vector.
Python ollama SDK: register an add(a, b) Python function as a tool for a chat with llama3.1, run the tool call that comes back, and print the result.
Write an asyncio Python script that uses ollama's async client to ask two different local models the same question concurrently and prints both answers.
